#poweringyouringenuity\u00c2\u00a0 <\/strong>\u00f0\u009f\u009a\u0080<\/p>

Our mission is to bridge top-level technology companies with engineering talent from across the globe. With presence in LATAM, USA and Europe, we empower companies by providing remote engineering teams of all levels tailored to the needs of each project.<\/p>

<\/p>

Our teams are passionate about technology and thrive on challenges. We value technical expertise and a willingness to learn new things. Each development is tailored to the needs of each project, so being passionate about learning and using new languages, tools, and frameworks is part of our DNA. Our software engineering teams focus on best coding practices to ensure readability, reusability, and scalability of our systems' designs and developments.<\/p>

<\/p>

Culture & People Development Specialist<\/h1>

At Ekumen, culture and growth go hand in hand. We believe high-performing teams are built on continuous learning, meaningful feedback, and a strong sense of belonging.<\/p>

We\u00e2\u0080\u0099re looking for a Culture & People Development Specialist to help us scale that experience intentionally. This role will play a key part in shaping the employee journey, from development frameworks and performance processes to engagement initiatives and cultural evolution across our distributed teams.<\/p>

If you\u00e2\u0080\u0099re passionate about building thriving workplaces where people and business grow together, this is your opportunity.<\/p>


<\/p>

What You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll Do<\/h2>

  • Partner with business leaders to align people development initiatives with organizational goals.<\/p><\/li>

  • Lead performance and feedback processes, continuously improving how we grow and develop our teams.<\/p><\/li>

  • Design and evolve training strategies, career frameworks, and progression paths.<\/p><\/li>

  • Drive engagement, internal communication, recognition, and DEI initiatives across distributed teams.<\/p><\/li>

  • Leverage data and insights to strengthen retention, performance, and employee experience.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

    What We\u00e2\u0080\u0099re Looking For<\/h2>

    • 5+ years of experience in culture, engagement, learning & development, or similar roles (experience in tech\/software environments is a strong plus).<\/p><\/li>

    • Bachelor\u00e2\u0080\u0099s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.<\/p><\/li>

    • Experience working with distributed, remote-first, or hybrid global teams.<\/p><\/li>

    • Strong execution capability: you can move from strategy to implementation.<\/p><\/li>

    • Data-driven mindset with experience using engagement and performance metrics.<\/p><\/li>

    • Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate cross-functionally.<\/p><\/li>

    • Strategic thinking with a clear understanding of how people initiatives impact business results.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

      Why Join Us?<\/h2>

      You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll help shape how our people grow, connect, and succeed. Your work will directly impact engagement, retention, and the evolution of our culture, influencing not just processes, but everyday experiences.<\/p>

      If you\u00e2\u0080\u0099re excited about building systems that empower people and elevate performance in a fast-growing, distributed tech environment, we\u00e2\u0080\u0099d love to meet you.<\/p>

      Join us to be part of a dynamic community where your skills and contributions truly matter!<\/p>Please mention the word **TOUGHEST** and tag RMTgyLjE4NC4xNjQuMjQ2 when applying to show you read the job post completely (#RMTgyLjE4NC4xNjQuMjQ2).       About HealthMatch<\/strong><\/h2>

      HealthMatch connects patients with clinical trials. 80% of trials are delayed because they can't find enough participants \u00e2\u0080\u0094 we're closing that gap with technology. We have over 2M patients in our database and a lean team of fewer than 20 building something that matters.<\/p>

      The Role<\/strong><\/h2>

      HealthMatch works with sponsors and clinical trial sites to recruit and enroll participants into studies. This role is the human link in that chain. You're the person sites hear from when referrals come in. You're the person patients talk to when they're considering a trial. You're the reason referrals turn into enrollments instead of going cold.<\/p>

      Day to day, you'll manage relationships with dozens of trial sites across LatAm and US markets, follow up with patients, keep our CRM accurate, and be trained to review medical records alongside our Medical Director. The work is phone-heavy, email-heavy, and detail-heavy.<\/p>

      Over time, we want you to do more than execute \u00e2\u0080\u0094 we want you to spot what's broken and help fix it. If you notice a follow-up workflow that could be automated, or a site engagement pattern that should become a playbook, we want you to raise it and help build it.<\/p>

      <\/p>

      What a Typical Week Looks Like<\/strong><\/h2>

      Most weeks, the majority of your time is outbound communication:<\/p>

      • Calling and emailing clinical trial sites to make sure patient referrals are being followed up on<\/p><\/li>

      • Tracking referral statuses across our portal and CRM - this means real-time data entry, not batch updates at the end of the week<\/p><\/li>

      • Supporting patients directly as they move through enrollment<\/p><\/li>

      • Flagging sites that aren't engaging and working with the team on how to re-engage them<\/p><\/li>

      • Helping onboard new sites to the HealthMatch platform<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

        Some weeks will also include:<\/p>

        • Working with our Medical Director on medical record reviews (you'll be trained on this)<\/p><\/li>

        • Building or refining parts of our site engagement playbook based on what you're learning in real conversations<\/p><\/li>

        • Jumping into other company needs as they come up - we're a small team and the edges of everyone's role are blurry<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

          Who This Role Is For<\/strong><\/h2>

          You must be fluent in both Spanish and English.<\/strong> This is non-negotiable - you'll be working across US and LatAm markets daily.<\/p>

          You're great on the phone.<\/strong> Not in a scripted, call-center way. You build trust quickly with site coordinators and patients, you're comfortable with follow-up that feels repetitive, and you understand that strong relationships are what make the repetition bearable. The better your relationships get, the more the job opens up.<\/p>

          You're organized under volume.<\/strong> You'll be managing dozens of site relationships simultaneously. Dropping a follow-up means a patient might not get into a trial. You need a system, whether that's meticulous CRM hygiene, your own tracking spreadsheet, or something else, and the discipline to maintain it.<\/p>

          You have at least 2 years of professional experience.<\/strong> We're less concerned about where and more concerned that you've worked in a fast-paced, accountable environment before. Experience at a clinical research site or a background in health\/science is a plus, it'll shorten your ramp, but it's not required.<\/p>

          You're resourceful.<\/strong> When something is inefficient, you'd rather figure out a fix than complain about it. That might mean building yourself a dashboard, learning a new tool, or making a clear case to our product team about what needs to change. We don't expect you to write code, but we do expect you to think like someone who hates waste.<\/p>

          What's Hard About This Job<\/strong><\/h2>

          We're being direct because we'd rather you opt out now than burn out in three months.<\/p>

          Some weeks are 80% phone and email follow-up with limited variety. Working with sites that don't want to engage is genuinely frustrating \u00e2\u0080\u0094 you'll need persistence and thick skin. The role requires strong attention to detail during work that can feel monotonous. And as a startup with fewer than 20 people, you'll regularly be asked to help with things that aren't in this job description.<\/p>

          We're looking for someone who sees the line between their follow-up call and a patient getting access to a trial, and uses that connection to push through the hard days.<\/p>

          Compensation & Details<\/strong><\/h2>

          • Remote role, no relocation required<\/p><\/li>

          • Preference to be in the North East and willing to travel, while we are remote we often get together in either New York, Boston, or Durham NC for co-working sessions<\/p><\/li>

          • Primary working timezone is EST, but as a global business we often have meetings with our team in Sydney Australia<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

            How to Apply<\/strong><\/h2>

            1. Online application<\/strong> with long form answers<\/p><\/li>

            2. 30-minute first round interview <\/strong>with the hiring manager + director of customer success<\/p><\/li>

            3. Paid take-home project<\/strong> (~2 hours, $50 gift card to say thanks for your time)<\/p><\/li>

            4. Final interview <\/strong>with CEO & Founder (in person in Boston, expenses covered)<\/p><\/li><\/ol>

              HealthMatch is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. If this role sounds like you, we'd encourage you to apply, even if you don't check every box.<\/p>

              <\/p>

              PLEASE NOTE:<\/strong><\/h2>

              This role was recently advertised.               Who we are<\/strong><\/p>

              We're Redis. We built the product that runs the fast apps our world runs on. (If you checked the weather, used your credit card, or looked at your flight status online today, you\u00e2\u0080\u0099re welcome.) At Redis, you\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll work with the fastest, simplest technology in the business\u00e2\u0080\u0094whether you\u00e2\u0080\u0099re building it, telling its story, or selling it to our 10,000+ worldwide customers. We\u00e2\u0080\u0099re creating a faster world with simpler experiences. You in?<\/p>

              Why would you love this job?<\/strong><\/p>

              As a Technical Support Engineer, you will be responsible for helping customers by diagnosing and resolving complex technical issues in a high-contribution role with exciting technical challenges, ongoing learning, and the excitement of helping name-brand customers as part of our fun, tight-knit team.<\/p>

              In this role, you will use and extend your existing technical depth and increase your technical breadth by addressing complex problems for the top companies in the world. You will level up to be an expert complex problem solver on Redis Enterprise Software, being used as a high-performance database by thousands of worldwide customers. You will dive deep into different exciting forefront technologies by supporting Redis Enterprise running on the top Cloud Platforms and in the top container orchestration platforms.<\/p>

              Join the best of the best and continuously learn new things. We are looking for brilliant experts who are curious, persistent, and happy digging through the full stack, from code to Sysadmin to networking to performance. If this sounds like you, please check out the technical foundation we\u00e2\u0080\u0099d like you to bring.<\/p>

              What you\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll do:<\/strong><\/p>

              • Work with customers to troubleshoot and resolve complex software issues:<\/p>

                • Reproduce issues, replicating customer environments as needed.<\/p><\/li>

                • Document issues and contribute to our internal team documentation.<\/p><\/li>

                • Provide Root Cause Analysis<\/p><\/li><\/ul><\/li>

                • Collaborate with Engineering as needed to provide solutions.<\/p><\/li>

                • Analyze performance questions that may arise along the data path (including networks) for deployments that may be in the Cloud or On-premises.<\/p><\/li>

                • Provide technical expertise during testing, deployment, and upgrading of Redis software.<\/p><\/li>

                • Manage critical customer issues, facilitating communication between customers, CloudOps, Engineering, Product, TAMs, and Sales.<\/p><\/li>

                • Serve as the customer advocate for timely resolution of issues and handling escalations while helping customers realize and maximize the value of their Redis subscription.<\/p><\/li>

                • Participate in new product development, customer training, and other support-related activities.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                  This role requires a 5-day work week that includes Saturday and Sunday.<\/p>

                  What will you need to have?<\/strong><\/p>

                  • At least five years of technical experience as a Support Engineer, Systems Engineer, Software Engineer, or Site Reliability Engineer in an enterprise software company<\/p><\/li>

                  • At least four years of experience troubleshooting real-time production systems<\/p><\/li>

                  • At least two years of hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure.<\/p><\/li>

                  • Strong background in scripting or programming languages (Python, Java, C#, JavaScript, Bash, Powershell, etc.)<\/p><\/li>

                  • Expert working knowledge in Linux\/Unix and networking (TCP\/IP)<\/p><\/li>

                  • Professional experience working with networking tools like wireshark, tcpdump, etc.<\/p><\/li>

                  • Experience in analyzing and debugging production issues at scale.<\/p><\/li>

                  • Experience with alerting and monitoring systems (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Splunk, etc.).<\/p><\/li>

                  • Working knowledge of Cloud-based and On-premises environments<\/p><\/li>

                  • Proficiency in communication and presentation, both written and verbal (in English)<\/p><\/li>

                  • Strong technical background with excellent problem-solving and multi-tasking skills<\/p><\/li>

                  • High availability and commitment to customers at any time<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                    Extra great if you have:<\/strong><\/p>

                    • Bachelor of Science in Computer Science or Information Systems<\/p><\/li>

                    • Experience with NoSQL databases (especially Redis)<\/p><\/li>

                    • Experience working with container orchestration environments, such as Kubernetes<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                      Salary: $12,000 - $15,000 USD a year
                      Job Type: Full-time
                      <\/p>

                      At LaunchBrightly, we are on a mission to automate a process that continually captures and enhances screenshots of all your SaaS product features<\/a>. The continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI\/CD) pipeline and process that engineers adopted over the last decade, is what we ultimately want to build for customer support teams. TLDR; LaunchBrightly = Github Actions\/Circle CI for customer support. And we are currently looking to bring onboard a strong Implementation Specialist.<\/p>

                      Everyone at LaunchBrightly is a creator, an owner, or perhaps better, a celebration of the crazy ones. The misfits. The rebels. Most big companies are afraid of entrusting the future of the company and their software to a few makers, and as they defend against that, they end up with three layers of managers and a product designed by a committee. We want the opposite. We need the opposite as we enter virgin territory trying to build that CI\/CD pipeline and process for the support organization. As our customer pushes a new feature to production, we automatically update all product images within the help center. This is a thrilling adventure!<\/p>

                      Our work is remote, not distant. We believe that being a remote-first organization allows us to build an exceptional team composed of makers and sellers with diverse backgrounds and skill sets from around the globe. Our default work culture is asynchronous and emphasizes written communication to prioritize maker time, focus and company productivity. Our goal is not to eliminate face-to-face interaction, but we should default to a setting where written documentation is so strong that a meeting on most project subject matters would seem odd.\u00c2\u00a0\u00c2\u00a0<\/p>

                      Founded in 2022, and backed by prominent venture firms including IA Ventures, Tuesday Capital, and B Capital Group, this is a unique opportunity to join this band of misfits on the ground floor. As an early teammate you will be working intimately with the founding team, who has done 0 to 1 five times over while seeing four of them come to an exit.<\/p>

                      The Implementation Specialist will become an integral member of an intimate 10-person team consisting of:<\/p>

                      • Maker #9 (Implementation Specialist, You!)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #8 (Technical Product Manager, You!)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #7 (Esau, Engineer)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #6 (Melissa, Senior Implementation Specialist)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #5 (Michel, Engineer)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #4 (Lucas, Engineer \/ UX)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #3 (Jose, Engineer)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #2 (Hyder, Backend Engineer)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Maker #1 (Dennis<\/a>, Founder)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Seller #1 (Josh<\/a>, Co-founder)<\/p><\/li>

                      • Helper #1 (Vibeke)<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                        However, we are looking not just for a strong Implementation Specialist but, rather, a teammate we see as integral to LaunchBrightly\u00e2\u0080\u0099s long-term journey and vital to our success. Someone who is comfortable in the dark and who sees a certain romance, like we do, in embracing the unknowns that come with being on the startup journey. We have a track record of seeing those colleagues who join us on the ground floor of our ventures ride the startup journey to completion (exit) with us, as we grow and become successful together. This is something we take seriously, and something we are extremely proud of!\u00c2\u00a0<\/p>

                        Job Description<\/h2>

                        This role is focused on creating Screenshot Automation Recipes for customer implementations using our internal Screenshot Recipe Builder<\/a> (our primary customer implementation tool).\u00c2\u00a0<\/p>

                        Your primary role will be to focus on customer implementations. You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll spend the majority of your time designing and building automation recipes at scale \u00e2\u0080\u0094 often creating many hundreds per month \u00e2\u0080\u0094 ensuring customer help centers stay visually accurate and up-to-date.<\/p>

                        While this is not a customer-facing role, your work directly shapes the customer experience. You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll act as a key bridge between implementation execution, product design, and product quality, helping us build an experience that both works at scale today and evolves toward a future where non-technical users can self-serve with ease.\u00c2\u00a0<\/p>

                        Over time, you\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll develop a deep intuition for how implementation should work \u00e2\u0080\u0094 not just how it works today \u00e2\u0080\u0094 and play a key role in shaping that future.<\/p>

                        You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll be responsible for things such as:<\/p>

                        • Create and maintain large volumes of Screenshot Automation Recipes across customer environments<\/p><\/li>

                        • Translate real-world product UIs into reliable, repeatable automation workflows<\/p><\/li>

                        • Continuously improve the quality, consistency and resilience of recipes<\/p><\/li>

                        • Analyze feedback from users, and identify inefficiencies as you set up recipes to suggest areas of improvement<\/p><\/li>

                        • Act as a frontline QA partner for the implementation experience, identifying bugs, edge cases and inconsistencies while building recipes<\/p><\/li>

                        • Use tools like the Browser Console, lightweight scripting when needed, and our Product Screenshots and Help Center Sync API<\/a> as QA tools; Postman will be your best friend<\/p><\/li>

                        • Identify friction in the implementation workflow as you use the product daily, and suggest improvements to the Recipe Builder and broader implementation experience<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                          We are looking for previous experience working with<\/h2>

                          • Strong understanding of HTML, CSS and the DOM (and being able to manipulate elements at will in the console)<\/p><\/li>

                          • Ability to inspect, debug, and clearly document frontend behavior<\/p><\/li>

                          • Comfort working in browser dev tools to diagnose and validate issues<\/p><\/li>

                          • API experience, and specifically work in Postman; Consuming and commenting on internal APIs<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                            What you bring to the table<\/h2>

                            • A customer-first mentality, with an instinct to imagine how users will react and interact<\/p><\/li>

                            • Strong and precise communication and documentation skills, with a will to understand and be understood<\/p><\/li>

                            • A strong product instinct \u00e2\u0080\u0094 you naturally think about how users experience what you\u00e2\u0080\u0099re building<\/p><\/li>

                            • High attention to detail and a bias toward quality and correctness<\/p><\/li>

                            • The personal organization skills and patience to manage multiple contexts as projects and features come together. A good Implementation Specialist can do things like: juggle and monitor open Git tickets and enhancements being worked on by the Engineering team, keep zero inbox, write notes for themselves to keep their small tasks straight, whip up shell scripts\/test data to automate repetitive tasks<\/p><\/li>

                            • A passion for being part of the startup journey and taking ownership of the company\u00e2\u0080\u0099s success<\/p><\/li>

                            • A willingness to take initiative and enthusiasm to make an impact and take action<\/p><\/li>

                            • Thrives in a highly collaborative environment and enthusiasm for being a team player<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                              Bonus point if you<\/h2>

                              • Have experience in QA, product operations, or implementation-heavy roles<\/p><\/li>

                              • Have contributed to product design decisions or UX improvements<\/p><\/li>

                              • Have worked closely with Engineering teams and written detailed tickets\/specs<\/p><\/li>

                              • Have built or have contributed to building something from 0-1<\/p><\/li>

                              • Are someone technically-minded, and who derives satisfaction from producing a genuinely useful product that improves the lives of others<\/p><\/li>

                              • Have experience working on tools used by non-technical users (e.g. knowledge bases, CMS, internal tools)<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                                What we offer<\/h2>

                                • Flexible working hours and locations. Your success is measured on outcomes<\/p><\/li>

                                • A culture of NO meetings where maker time and productivity is prioritized<\/p><\/li>

                                • Excellent start-up culture with openness and inclusiveness<\/p><\/li>

                                • True ownership and autonomy over your role and the impact on the company<\/p><\/li>

                                • Prioritization of focus time and productivity\u00c2\u00a0<\/p><\/li>

                                • Your choice of technical setup and equipment<\/p><\/li>

                                • Experienced founders with many years of successfully building product across multiple ventures<\/p><\/li>

                                • Annual meetup as a team in New York City<\/p><\/li><\/ul>

                                  What to expect in the interview process<\/h2>

                                  Given the remote nature of our work setting, one of the core principles for how we operate as a remote team is written communication. And we therefore run a slightly unique interview process that begins with an email-based exchange to learn more about your background, communication style and overall fit for the role.<\/p>

                                  • Stage 1:<\/strong> You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll begin with an email conversation with our Co-founder, Josh Peacock<\/a>, to get a sense of your technical background, written communication, and English proficiency.<\/p><\/li>

                                  • Stage 2:<\/strong>\u00c2\u00a0 We\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll then ask you to complete a shor``t technical assignment to demonstrate your practical skills in action.<\/p><\/li>

                                  • Final stage:<\/strong> You\u00e2\u0080\u0099ll have two video calls \u00e2\u0080\u0094 one with our Founder, Dennis Mortensen<\/a>, and another with our Senior Implementation Specialist to give us a chance to connect more personally and explore how you'd fit into the team.<\/p><\/li><\/ul>
